title: Trouble On The Selection
content:
آ آ آ آ You lazy boy, youï؟½re here at last,
آ آ آ آ You must be wooden-legged;
آ آ آ آ Now, are you sure the gate is fast
آ آ آ آ And all the sliprails pegged
آ آ آ آ And all the milkers at the yard,
آ آ آ آ The calves all in the pen?
آ آ آ آ We donï؟½t want Poleyï؟½s calf to suck
آ آ آ آ His mother dry again.

آ آ آ آ And did you mend the broken rail
آ آ آ آ And make it firm and neat?
آ آ آ آ I sï؟½pose you want that brindle steer
آ آ آ آ All night among the wheat.
آ آ آ آ And if he finds the lucerne patch,
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½ll stuff his belly full;
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½ll eat till he gets ï؟½blownï؟½ on that
آ آ آ آ And busts like Ryanï؟½s bull.

آ آ آ آ Old Spot is lost? Youï؟½ll drive me mad,
آ آ آ آ You will, upon my soul!
آ آ آ آ She might be in the boggy swamps
آ آ آ آ Or down a diggerï؟½s hole.
آ آ آ آ You neednï؟½t talk, you never looked
آ آ آ آ Youï؟½d find her if youï؟½d choose,
آ آ آ آ Instead of poking ï؟½possum logs
آ آ آ آ And hunting kangaroos.

آ آ آ آ How came your boots as wet as muck?
آ آ آ آ You tried to drown the ants!
آ آ آ آ Why donï؟½t you take your bluchers off,
آ آ آ آ Good Lord, heï؟½s tore his pants!
آ آ آ آ Your fatherï؟½s coming home to-night;
آ آ آ آ Youï؟½ll catch it hot, youï؟½ll see.
آ آ آ آ Now go and wash your filthy face
آ آ آ آ And come and get your tea.
meaning: About: Henry Lawson (17 June 1867 - 2 September 1922) was an Australian writer and poet. Along with his contemporary Banjo Paterson, Lawson is among the best-known Australian poets and fiction writers of the colonial period.
